_meta.description: Domain contract objects: canonical resource, primary table, lifecycle status, workflow state model.
_meta.usage: Find which domain owns a resource and which table stores it. Use primary_table for SQL queries.

all_contracts.purpose: Provide read-only snapshot truth for inventory position by site, warehouse, lot, and lineage so KPI, audit, and exception review do not query mutable transaction tables directly.
Provide read-only OEE projection by equipment and shift so performance review, loss analysis, and gate meetings use stable snapshot truth rather than recalculating from mutable execution events.
Provide read-only plant KPI snapshots so lean review, management review, and continuous-improvement boards consume a stable multi-signal projection with preserved lineage.
Provide read-only BOM snapshot truth per production order so planning, costing, and traceability review can inspect the released material structure without re-querying mutable planning records.
Provide read-only route snapshot truth per production order so scheduling, traceability, and execution analysis can inspect the released routing structure without depending on mutable route masters.
Control customer complaint, follow-up, escalation, and closure so service issues remain governed and traceable instead of disappearing into touchpoint notes.
Capture customer commitment as governed demand so commercial promise, SO linkage, and downstream delivery remain traceable from the first accepted PO.
Control commercial estimation, review, offer release, and conversion so customer demand does not bypass governed quote truth before SO commitment.
Capture structured quote win-loss and effectiveness analysis so commercial learning, margin review, and continuous improvement are preserved as governed assessment records instead of ad hoc notes.
Turn accepted commercial demand into governed revenue-producing execution demand spanning engineering, production release, shipment gate, and financial realization.
Control AP three-way match, hold, posting, payment, and close so procurement correction cannot bypass financial truth.
Represent customer receivable truth from shipment or billing release through dispute and settlement, without depending on a mixed AP/AR legacy object.
Control governed posting-date exceptions so backdated financial or logistics adjustments cannot bypass closed-period discipline.
Represent governed downward financial adjustments so AP and AR corrections remain explicit and auditable instead of hiding inside a mixed invoice object.
Represent governed upward financial adjustments so AP and AR correction exposure stays explicit, traceable, and separate from mixed invoice truth.
Represent capitalization decisions that convert spend into governed asset value so maintenance, finance, and asset master stay aligned.
Provide read-only inventory valuation projection per item, site, date, and valuation method so finance review and period close consume stable valuation truth with preserved lineage.
Control accounting period close and reopen decisions so posting gates remain explicit, auditable, and separable from invoice records.
Control MES-ERP mismatch handling so interface drift is triaged, compensated, and closed instead of patched outside governed integration flow.
Control return authorization, receipt, evaluation, disposition, and closure so reverse-logistics truth stays linked to quality and finance outcomes.
Control cycle-count scheduling and closure so stock accuracy is corrected through governed counting instead of ad hoc inventory adjustment.
Controls freight execution from booking through delivery closure so shipment release, carrier handoff, and proof-of-delivery stay under governed logistics truth.
Controls release, obsolescence, and inventory-usable truth for the shared item master without duplicating inventory item identity.
Controls movement posting, hold, and reversal so inventory truth remains reconcilable under corrections and audit.
Control outbound release, dispatch, delivery confirmation, and closure so OTIF and export-release truth stay governed from shipment creation to proof of delivery.
Provide read-only consolidated stock balance truth by item and warehouse so planning, cycle counting, and valuation can consume stable balance projections without mutating source transactions.
Controls warehouse release, hold, and archive so blocked storage areas cannot silently remain active for receiving, picking, or shipment.
Controls 5S audit planning, execution, follow-up, verification, and closure so visual-factory discipline becomes a governed lean loop instead of a score-only snapshot.
Control incident reporting, containment, investigation, corrective action, and closure so safety events cannot escape the EHS governance loop.
Controls preventive-maintenance plan approval, release, hold, and retirement so recurring PM cadence stays auditable before work orders are auto-released.
Control preventive and corrective maintenance execution so equipment intervention, spare usage, and closeout stay governed instead of being hidden in note-only maintenance history.
Controls permit submission, approval, activation, suspension, expiry, and closure so hazardous or regulated work cannot proceed outside a governed permit-to-work register.
Controls safety good-catch and hazard observations through assignment, containment, and closure so frontline EHS risks cannot disappear into note-only reporting.
Provide a canonical asset identity so finance, maintenance, and customer-installed asset references do not split into separate truths.
Controls obligation due, waiver, and closure so permits, audits, and regulatory actions have a governed compliance anchor.
Controls customer commercial release eligibility, order acceptance, and evidence retention on the customer master.
Controls labor qualification, access eligibility, and employee release into production and compliance scopes.
Controls equipment qualification, lockout, and usable-machine release before maintenance, calibration, and production execution.
Provide a canonical measuring-device contract so calibration release, quarantine, and metrology evidence stop drifting between tool-control naming and legacy gauge terminology.
Controls supplier qualification and spend authorization before purchasing or receiving release.
Control tool readiness, wear containment, and retirement before a tool can be issued to production, maintenance, or calibration use.
Capture machine-originated alarm and fault events with operator acknowledgement, escalation, downtime linkage, and maintenance follow-up without manually inventing machine data.
Controls shopfloor dispatch release, hold, and execution closure so operations cannot start or disappear from queue outside the governed MES release horizon.
Control in-process quality checks so process escapes trigger hold, reinspection, or NCR before final inspection and shipment release.
Translate confirmed sales demand into governed production intent so materials, routing, and downstream WO dispatch stay synchronized with commercial commitment.
Controls operation-level execution, hold, completion, and scrap so genealogy and in-process quality can anchor to the actual operation step.
Control factory plan review, publication, freeze, and completion so dispatch, capacity, and OTIF commitments all read the same released schedule truth.
Control executable shopfloor release, execution, and quality hold at the operation level so production truth is governed where work is actually performed.
Gate inbound material before stock release so supplier quality, quarantine, and NCR escalation are recorded from a governed incoming inspection object.
Control governed correction and reversal after receipt, IQC, or invoice impact so partial completion can be reconciled through traceable recovery instead of manual patches.
Control physical receipt, quarantine, acceptance, putaway, and reversal so stock and AP matching cannot outrun receiving truth.
Authorize buy demand before sourcing or PO release so procurement cannot bypass planning, budget, or gate controls.
Control inbound advance shipment notice, discrepancy handling, and receiving readiness so physical receipts cannot outrun supplier intent and dock planning truth.
Authorize governed external spend and supplier commitment so receiving, IQC, and AP matching cannot bypass approved procurement intent.
Control internal and supplier audit planning, execution, verification, and closure so compliance evidence and systemic findings remain governed from schedule through improvement follow-up.
Control temporary acceptance or shipment-release exception decisions so quality deviation from requirement cannot bypass governed approval and traceability.
Govern APQP/FMEA-linked control plans so product and process characteristics, control methods, reaction plans, approvals, revisions, and shop-floor inspection execution stay traceable.
Drive verified corrective and preventive action so recurring quality loss is closed through owned tasks, effectiveness review, and audit trace.
Control temporary process, product, or documentation deviations so exception handling remains explicit, time-bounded, and auditable instead of disappearing inside generic status notes.
Govern version control of QMS controlled documents (Manual, Policy, SOP, WI, Form, Annex, Handbook, JD). Distinct from Engineering Change Control (ECC = plm_change_*) which governs parts/BOMs/items.

Preserve the final-quality-control vocabulary used by operations while resolving it onto the governed outgoing-inspection truth so frontend and AI tooling stop treating FQC as a separate storage authority.
Control lean improvement intake, approval, implementation, verification, and closure so kaizen and systemic improvement work produces auditable benefit instead of unmanaged ideas.
Capture measurement-system-analysis and GRR evidence so acceptance of measurement capability is explicit before relying on data for release, SPC, or capability decisions.
Contain and disposition quality escapes from incoming, in-process, outgoing, or returned product so no defect signal disappears outside a governed NCR record.
Control outgoing quality release so finished goods cannot pass to shipment without governed final inspection truth.
Govern temporary override, waiver, and release bypass decisions so exceptional shipments or process gates remain explicit, timeboxed, signed, and reviewable.
Preserve structured statistical-process-control observations so process capability, rule violations, and escalation triggers are governed assessment evidence instead of spreadsheet-only history.
Provide forward and backward genealogy for lot, serial, batch, and production-order relationships so containment, recall, NCR, and customer-return decisions can be traced to source records.
